money/codes

  • Index
Search:
Group by:
  • Types
    • Alpha3
    • Currency
    • MoneyUnit
  • Consts
    • Currencies

Types

Alpha3 {.pure.} = enum
  DZD, AOA, ARS, AUD, AZN, BSD, BHD, THB, PAB, BBD, BYN, BZD, BMD, BTN, VEF,
  ESA, BOB, GBP, BND, BIF, XPF, XOF, XAF, CAD, KYD, CLP, CNX, COP, CRC, KMF,
  CDF, BAM, NIO, CUP, CZK, GMD, DKK, MKD, DJF, STD, NAD, DOP, VND, AMD, XCD,
  EGP, SVC, CVE, ETB, EUR, FKP, FJD, HUF, GHS, GIP, HTG, PYG, AWG, GNF, GYD,
  HKD, UAH, ISK, INR, IDR, IQD, JMD, JPY, JOD, KES, PGK, LAK, KWD, MWK, KGS,
  GEL, LBP, ALL, HNL, LSL, MDL, LRD, LYD, SZL, LTL, MGA, MYR, MVR, TMT, MUR,
  MXN, MAD, MZN, MMK, NGN, NPR, ANG, AFN, BGN, RUB, ILS, TWD, NZD, NOK, PEN,
  CNH, MRO, TOP, PKR, MOP, UYU, PHP, PLN, BWP, QAR, GTQ, ZAR, BRL, BYR, OMR,
  KHR, SAR, RON, RWF, KRW, RSD, SCR, SLE, SGD, SBD, SOS, SSP, LKR, SHP, SRD,
  SEK, CHF, TJS, BDT, WST, TZS, KZT, TTD, MNT, TND, TRY, AED, UGX, USD, UZS,
  VUV, YER, CNY, ZMW, noCurrency
The Alpha3 type is an enumeration of ISO 4217 currency codes. Each currency code is associated with its numeric code as defined by the ISO 4217 standard. For example, USD has the alpha-3 code "USD" and the numeric code 840. The noCurrency value represents an undefined or unknown currency. https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html 
Currency = tuple[name: string, alpha3: Alpha3, expo: int]
The Currency type represents a currency with its name, ISO 4217 alpha-3 code, and exponent. The expo field indicates the number of decimal places used by the currency (e.g., 2 for USD). 
MoneyUnit = enum
  moneyUnitCoins = "coins", moneyUnitNotes = "notes"
The MoneyUnit type represents the unit of money, which can be either coins or notes. By default, the unit is set to coins, but you can switch to notes by using the -d:moneyDefaultUnit=notes flag at compile-time.

Consts

Currencies: array[154, Currency] = [("ALGERIAN DINAR", Alpha3.DZD, 2),
                                    ("ANGOLA KWANZA", Alpha3.AOA, 2),
                                    ("ARGENTINE PESO", Alpha3.ARS, 2),
